# CLI Contract Checklist: Working Hours Window Configuration

**Purpose**: Validate that the CLI requirements for configurable working-hours windows are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation planning.
**Created**: 2026-06-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the exact custom working-hours flag names and value placeholders specified everywhere the CLI contract is described? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Contract §Inputs]
- [x] CHK002 Are both supported argument forms for custom working-hours flags documented, including separated values and equals-form values? [Completeness, Contract §Supported forms]
- [x] CHK003 Are the requirements complete for when custom working-hours flags are absent and the default window applies? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK004 Are lookup-only constraints complete for cases where working-hours flags are provided without `--compare`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Contract §Inputs]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements complete for applying the same configured window to both compared places in each place's local time? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is the `HH:mm` format defined clearly enough to exclude flexible values such as `9am`, `9:00`, `0900`, and invalid times such as `24:00`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Contract §Unsupported in v1]
- [x] CHK007 Are inclusive start and exclusive end boundaries stated unambiguously for both default and custom working-hours windows? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Data Model §WorkingHoursWindow]
- [x] CHK008 Is the meaning of "end must be later than start" clear for equal times, earlier end times, and overnight ranges? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Contract §Invalid working-hours range]
- [x] CHK009 Is the `Working hours window: <HH:mm>-<HH:mm>` output label specified with exact capitalization, spacing, separator, and value format? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Contract §Successful Comparison Output]
- [x] CHK010 Are working-hours error messages specified with enough detail for missing-pair, lookup-only, malformed-time, and invalid-range cases? [Clarity, Contract §Error Output]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the spec, data model, and CLI contract consistent about rejecting zero-length and overnight windows in v1? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §WorkingHoursWindow, Contract §Unsupported in v1]
- [x] CHK012 Are the spec, CLI contract, and quickstart consistent about working-hours validation taking precedence over place-resolution errors? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, Contract §Precedence, Quickstart §Validate Error Precedence]
- [x] CHK013 Are exit code requirements consistent with the existing v1 categories for success, invalid, unsupported, unknown, and ambiguous input? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-012, Contract §Exit Codes]
- [x] CHK014 Are successful lookup requirements consistently described as unchanged across spec, contract, and quickstart? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Contract §Successful Lookup Output]
- [x] CHK015 Are output-label stability requirements aligned between the spec and contract after adding `Working hours window`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016, Contract §Stability Requirements]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK016 Can the custom-window comparison acceptance scenarios be objectively evaluated from the documented labels and value formats? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1, Contract §Successful Comparison Output]
- [x] CHK017 Are default-window acceptance criteria measurable for both behavior preservation and required `Working hours window: 09:00-17:00` output? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 2, Contract §Default comparison value]
- [x] CHK018 Are invalid-input acceptance criteria measurable across missing-pair, malformed-time, zero-length, overnight, and lookup-only cases? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK019 Are success criteria traceable to the documented CLI contract changes rather than only to broad user outcomes? [Traceability, Spec §Success Criteria, Contract §Command Shape]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK020 Are primary comparison scenarios covered for custom windows that make both places suitable and only one place suitable? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK021 Are alternate scenarios covered for default working-hours behavior and unchanged single-place lookup behavior? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK022 Are exception scenarios covered for malformed values, missing paired flags, invalid ranges, and custom flags without comparison? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Contract §Error Output]
- [x] CHK023 Are mixed-error scenarios covered where working-hours arguments are invalid and place inputs are also unsupported, unknown, ambiguous, or invalid? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §Precedence]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK024 Are boundary cases explicitly defined for local time exactly equal to the custom start and exactly equal to the custom end? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §WorkingHoursAssessment]
- [x] CHK025 Are minute-precision custom windows such as `08:30-16:45` covered without introducing seconds or timezone-qualified working-hours values? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK026 Are requirements clear for a custom window that matches the default `09:00-17:00` window? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK027 Are offline and deterministic behavior requirements preserved for working-hours configuration without adding external APIs, persistent state, or network lookup? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK028 Are testability requirements specified for parsing, validation, boundary evaluation, comparison behavior, output labels, and error precedence? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK029 Are performance expectations still measurable after adding configurable working-hours behavior? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK030 Are assumptions explicit that the custom working-hours window is per-command and not a saved user preference? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §CustomWorkingHoursInput]
- [x] CHK031 Are assumptions explicit that timezone resolution, alias catalog behavior, UTC offsets, and signed time difference are unchanged? [Assumption, Spec §FR-008, Contract §Stability Requirements]
- [x] CHK032 Are constraints explicit that no target-framework, package, solution-structure, storage, or infrastructure changes are required? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Structure Decision]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK033 Are there any remaining conflicts between the spec saying output labels are preserved and the contract adding a new successful comparison label? [Conflict, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK034 Are there any remaining ambiguities about whether working-hours argument validation belongs to CLI parsing, domain validation, or both for requirement purposes? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK035 Is the requirements traceability sufficient for future tasks to map each CLI contract change to tests and implementation work? [Traceability, Spec §Functional Requirements, Contract §CLI Contract]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
